title: defer / panic / recover
subtitle: Read the runtime in order: register cleanup first, then watch return, panic unwind, and recover split into different paths.

panels.heading: Register cleanup right after acquire
panels.body: Nothing runs yet. `defer` only pushes cleanup onto the current function's stack.
bullets: The call is scheduled in LIFO order
Arguments are evaluated when defer is registered
state_lines: open file / lock mutex / begin span

panels.heading: Normal return drains the defer stack
panels.body: When the function returns normally, Go executes deferred calls before the frame is removed.
bullets: Named return values can still be changed inside deferred funcs
Cleanup runs even across multiple early returns
state_lines: function exits after deferred calls finish

panels.heading: Panic changes the path, not the rule
panels.body: A panic starts stack unwinding, but deferred calls in the panicking frame still run on the way out.
bullets: Deferred cleanup still executes during unwind
If nobody recovers, panic continues up and can crash the process

panels.heading: recover only works at the live boundary
panels.body: Recovery is only effective inside the immediate deferred function of the same goroutine that is currently panicking.
bullets: A helper that calls recover indirectly does not stop the panic
A parent goroutine cannot recover a child goroutine's panic
state_lines: defer func() { if r := recover(); r != nil { ... } }()
control resumes after deferred function returns

footer: Defer is about scheduled cleanup. Panic is about unwinding. Recover only works at one exact runtime boundary.
